text:
Clare Baldinghas been a familiar face on theBBCin recent weeks throughout the Olympics – but she has a "horrifying" past.

Balding has been in the presenter's chair during many of the unforgettable moments from Paris on the BBC's coverage, with Mark Chapman and Gabby Logan also on duty at various stages.

Clare, a veteran broadcaster whose resume spans the BBC,Channel 4, and BT Sport, is especially familiar to audiences, having delivered coverage for major events like the Grand National, theCommonwealth Games, and Wimbledon.

READ MORE:Huw Edwards makes ex-junior BBC employee who received 'inappropriate messages' 'feel sick'

READ MORE:Prince Harry net worth boost as he's 'to inherit fortune in weeks' - dwarfing Prince William's share

Though Clare's on-screen charm has won the hearts of viewers, her off-camera life hasn't escaped public fascination, making headlines over time. Beloved as she may be, Clare still reflects on her challenging school days, marked by an unfortunate shoplifting incident that continues to trouble her memory.

Speaking on Gyles Brandreth's Rosebud podcast, she shared: "I got into a lot of trouble when I was young. When I first went off to boarding school, I think I felt an enormous pressure to be one of the crowd. So I think I was very susceptible to peer pressure, so getting in with the 'in-gang' required doing quite daring things, one of which was shoplifting. And of course, I got caught, and I got suspended at only the beginning of my second year at school."

Clare shared: "I remember that being so shameful, because also when I rang home my parents weren't there. So Vicky, who was my father's terrifying secretary - the scariest woman... think of all those films. She came to get me. That was absolutely horrifying and the shame of it... I thought I was going to be expelled. So that was awful."

The popular presenter disclosed in 2009 that she had been diagnosed with thyroid cancer after spotting a lump on her neck during a TV appearance. The illness can manifest symptoms such as a persistent lump in the neck, ongoingsore throat, and difficulty swallowing.

She endured radioactive iodine treatment to combat the disease, following surgery which removed a cyst, her entire thyroid gland and a lymph node. Despite fears about how it might affect her voice, Clare was jubilantly given the all-clear by the end of 2010. "I had to have a little operation just before Christmas to take out a nasty little lymph node and there was a worry that I might have to have more treatment," she confided.

Happily sharing the good news, she continued: "But I had a lovely letter from my oncologist just after Christmas saying, 'Hurrah, no more treatment', so I'm very happy about that. 2011 is going to be a good one."

title: Clare Balding's 'horrifying' past as BBC Olympics presenter shares 'awful' memories - Irish Mirror
author: Daniel Bird
description: Clare Balding is one of the UK's most popular TV presenters, having worked on some of the biggest events in the sporting calendar, but away from the cameras she's also had to deal with some difficult moments
